description_long: This is a rustic wooden cabin with a steeply pitched, reddish-brown gable roof featuring two skylights and a stone chimney. The exterior walls are constructed from timber logs, complemented by a stone foundation and stone-clad columns supporting the overhanging roof. A prominent wooden balcony with a railing extends across the front facade, above a set of large glass doors, while the rear side features a smaller covered entryway. The windows are rectangular and symmetrically arranged, providing ample natural light to the interior. The cabin sits on a dark, flat base that could represent a patio or foundation slab. Architectural details such as exposed beams, stonework, and woodwork evoke a cozy, alpine lodge aesthetic.
description: A rustic wooden cabin with a steep gable roof, stone foundation, and a prominent front balcony above large glass doors. Timber walls, stone columns, and symmetrical skylights and windows give it an alpine lodge appearance.
description_view_0: The rear view shows the steep gable roof with two skylights and a small covered entryway supported by stone columns.
description_view_1: This angled side view highlights the wooden balcony, stone chimney, and the detailed timber and stone construction.
description_view_2: The front view displays the main entrance with a wide balcony above, three large glass doors, and symmetrical roof skylights.
description_view_3: This angled front view emphasizes the balcony, glass doors, and the overhanging roof supported by stone and timber columns.
attribute_roof_type: steep gable roof with wide overhangs, two skylights, and a stone chimney; covered with reddish-brown shingles
attribute_window_arrangement: Four rectangular skylights symmetrically placed on the roof, three large glass doors/windows grouped together on the front facade, and additional windows on the side walls; arrangement is mostly symmetrical
attribute_door_placement: A set of three large glass doors centered on the front facade beneath the balcony; a smaller entry door at the rear under the roof overhang
